title: Criticality on Rényi defects at (2+1)$d$ O(3) quantum critical points
authors: Yanzhang Zhu, Zhe Wang, Meng Cheng, Zheng Yan
abstract: At a quantum critical point, the universal scaling behavior of Rényi entanglement entropy is controlled by the universality class of the codimension-two Rényi (or conical) defects in the infrared theory. In this work we perform a systematic study of critical correlations along Rényi defect lines in (2+1)d quantum spin models realizing quantum phase transitions described by the O(3) Wilson-Fisher universality class, using large-scale quantum Monte Carlo simulations. We present numerical evidence that, for a fixed Rényi index $n$, there exist multiple Rényi defect universality classes, with distinct critical exponents for the O(3) order parameter on the defect. These universality classes are realized by choosing microscopically different entanglement cuts in lattice models, which we classify as ordinary, special and extraordinary according to their relation to surface criticality. For the extraordinary entanglement cut, we further find evidence for a phase transition on the defect as a function of the Rényi index. Our results highlight the key role of defect universality classes in determining the universal scaling of Rényi entropy, and provide a framework for understanding the previously observed dependence of Rényi entropy scaling on microscopic lattice details.
summary: This paper demonstrates that the universality of Renyi entanglement entropy is determined by the specific geometry of the entanglement cut, which can lead to different defect universality classes. It identifies a phase transition on the 'extraordinary' defect line driven by the Renyi index. This provides a framework to understand why entanglement scaling can appear sensitive to microscopic lattice details.
main_problem: The study investigates why the universal scaling of Renyi entanglement entropy depends on the microscopic details of the entanglement cut and explores the universality classes of codimension-two Renyi defects at (2+1)d O(3) quantum critical points.
main_result: The authors find that different entanglement cuts realize distinct Renyi defect universality classes (ordinary, special, and extraordinary) and provide evidence for a phase transition on the extraordinary defect as a function of the Renyi index n.
method: Large-scale quantum Monte Carlo simulations on n-sheeted replica manifolds are used to measure local observables and extract scaling dimensions via finite-size scaling analysis.
model_or_system: The study uses (2+1)d quantum spin models at the O(3) Wilson-Fisher quantum critical point, specifically the 1/2 columnar dimerized Heisenberg model and the spin-1/2 bilayer Heisenberg model.
key_observables: Renyi-n expectation values, defect spin-spin correlation functions, and the defect Binder cumulant.
important_parameters: Renyi index n, system size L, and the coupling ratio J'/J used to reach the critical point.
main_assumptions: Numerical access is restricted to positive integer n > 1 via the replica approach; the behavior of the special defect remains somewhat inconclusive due to subtle numerical signals.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows schematic entanglement cuts (ordinary, special, extraordinary); Figure 2 shows finite-size scaling for ordinary/special cuts; Figure 3 shows scaling for the extraordinary defect; Figure 4 shows the Binder cumulant crossing for the extraordinary cut.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of entanglement cut sensitivity, describes the quantum spin models and replica QMC method, presents results for different defect types (ordinary, special, extraordinary), discusses the phase transition in the extraordinary case, and concludes with implications for entanglement entropy scaling.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic illustration of the columnar dimerized Heisenberg model and entanglement bipartitions. Thick blue bonds represent the strong couplings J′, and thin black bonds denote the weak couplings J. The dashed line indicates the entanglement bipartition, which separates the system into subsystem A (in black) and its environment ¯A (in white). Panels (a) and (b) correspond to ordinary and special cuts, while panel (c) corresponds to the extraordinary cut realized by a tilted cut in the same model. These three cuts are used throughout the paper to realize the three classes of Rényi de- fects compared below.

FIG. 2. Finite-size scaling of observables for ordinary and spe- cial Rényi defects at n = 2 in the columnar dimerized Heisen- berg model. Left panels: ordinary; right panels: special. Top row shows the spin correlation Cs(L) on the Rényi line defects, and bottom row shows the Binder cumulant U2(L). System sizes range from L = 16 to 80. Solid lines are fits described in the text.

FIG. 3. Finite-size scaling of observables for the extraordinary Rényi defect at different Rényi indices. Columns (left to right) correspond to n = 2, n = 4, and n = 10. Top row shows the spin correlation Cs(L) on the Rényi defect line, and bottom row shows the Binder cumulant U2(L). For n = 4 and n = 10, we additionally show the squared magnetization M 2 z . System sizes range from L = 16 to 80. Solid lines are fits described in the text.

FIG. 4. Binder cumulant U2 as a function of Rényi index n for (a) ordinary, (b) special, and (c) extraordinary Rényi defects in the columnar dimerized Heisenberg model. Differ- ent symbols correspond to system sizes L = 16, 24, 32. For the ordinary and special cuts, U2 evolves smoothly with n and no crossing is observed, consistent with the absence of a finite-n phase transition. In contrast, for the extraordinary cut the Binder cumulant curves cross near nc ≈3–4, indicat- ing a transition between a disordered regime at small n and an ordered regime at larger n.
